title: Seinfeld
description: A stand-up comedian and his quirky group of friends navigate the absurdities of everyday life in New York City.

title: Cheers
description: Set in a Boston bar where everybody knows your name, Cheers follows the relationships and adventures of a colorful group of locals.

title: The Office
description: A mockumentary following the employees of Dunder Mifflin Paper Company in Scranton, Pennsylvania.

title: Game of Thrones
description: Nine noble families fight for control of the mythical land of Westeros while an ancient enemy returns after being dormant for thousands of years.

title: Ted Lasso
description: An American college football coach is hired to manage an English soccer team despite having no knowledge of the sport.

title: Schitts Creek
description: A formerly wealthy family is forced to relocate to the small town they once purchased as a joke.

title: Curb Your Enthusiasm
description: Larry David, co-creator of Seinfeld, plays a fictionalized version of himself in this unscripted comedy about the minutiae of everyday life.

title: The White Lotus
description: A social satire set at an exclusive tropical resort, following different guests and staff over the span of a week.

title: Parks and Recreation
description: The absurd antics of an ambitious, relentlessly optimistic mid-level bureaucrat and her team in the Parks Department of Pawnee, Indiana.

title: Animal Kingdom
description: A seventeen-year-old goes to live with his mother's family, the Codys, a Southern California crime family led by his matriarchal grandmother.

title: The Muppet Show
description: Kermit the Frog and his ensemble of Muppet performers stage a variety show while coping with the chaos that inevitably follows.

title: Supernatural
description: Two brothers follow their father's footsteps as hunters, fighting evil supernatural beings of many kinds including monsters, demons, and gods that roam the earth.

title: The Boys
description: A group of vigilantes set out to take down corrupt superheroes who abuse their superpowers, while being hunted by Vought International.

episodes.title: Over the Hill with the Swords of a Thousand Men
Nothing Like It in the World
The Only Man in the Sky
The Last Time to Look on This World of Lies
Here Comes a Candle to Light You to Bed
We'll Keep the Red Flag Flying Here
Beware of the Jabberwock, My Son

description: A prequel to Yellowstone, following the Dutton family as they travel west through the Great Plains toward the last bastion of untamed America.

A prequel to Yellowstone following the Dutton family during the early twentieth century when pandemics, historic drought, the end of Prohibition, and the Great Depression all plague the mountain west.

episodes.title: A Cruel and Shallow Money Trench

title: Bates Motel
description: A contemporary prequel to Psycho, depicting the early life of Norman Bates and his deeply complex relationship with his mother Norma.

title: Spartacus: Blood and Sand
description: The epic saga of Spartacus, the Thracian gladiator who led a massive slave revolt against the Roman Republic.

title: Law & Order
description: The original procedural drama follows the New York City Police Department as they investigate crimes, and then the Manhattan District Attorney's office as they prosecute the offenders.

title: St. Denis Medical
description: A documentary-style comedy-drama set in an understaffed, overwhelmed Oregon hospital where the staff finds humor and humanity in the chaos of modern healthcare.

title: Frasier
description: Pompous but loveable psychiatrist Frasier Crane moves back to Seattle to host a radio call-in show after his marriage fails.

title: Malcolm in the Middle
description: A gifted young teen tries to survive life with his dimwitted but lovable family.

title: Modern Family
description: Three different but related families face trials and tribulations in their own unique ways.

title: Arrested Development
description: A wealthy family loses everything and the one son who has it together tries to keep them all united.

title: Scrubs
description: The lives of the staff and patients at Sacred Heart Hospital are followed from the point of view of an idealistic young doctor.

title: Barney Miller
description: Captain Barney Miller runs a New York City police precinct with his diverse and quirky team of detectives.

title: Sanford and Son
description: Fred Sanford and his son Lamont run a salvage business in the Watts neighborhood of Los Angeles.

title: The Bob Newhart Show
description: Chicago psychologist Bob Hartley navigates his professional and home life with deadpan wit.

title: Everybody Hates Chris
description: Based on Chris Rock's memories of growing up in the 1980s Brooklyn neighborhood of Bed-Stuy.

title: Abbott Elementary
description: A group of dedicated teachers -- and their wonderfully inappropriate principal -- navigate the Philadelphia public school system.

title: Ghosts
description: A young couple inherits a run-down mansion and discovers it is haunted by the ghosts of previous residents, only one of whom can see and hear them.

title: Superstore
description: A group of employees at a big-box store navigate their professional and personal lives in this workplace comedy.

title: The Middle
description: The Heck family struggles to survive in the middle of the country and the middle of life.

title: My Name Is Earl
description: A petty criminal wins the lottery and loses the ticket, convincing him karma is real -- so he sets out to make up for all the bad things he's done.

title: Kate & Allie
description: Two divorced women and their children share an apartment in New York City.

title: Superior Donuts
description: A Chicago donut shop owner and his young employee form an unlikely friendship as they navigate life in their changing neighborhood.

title: The McCarthys
description: A tight-knit Boston Irish-Catholic family navigates life, love, and basketball.

title: The Neighborhood
description: A friendly Midwesterner moves his family into a tough Los Angeles neighborhood, where his by-the-book attitude clashes with his new neighbor's urban sensibilities.

title: The Redd Foxx Comedy Hour
description: A variety comedy show hosted by Redd Foxx following his hit sitcom Sanford and Son, featuring his outrageous stand-up style and special guest stars.

title: Ozark
description: A financial advisor drags his family from Chicago to the Ozarks, where he must launder money to appease a drug boss.

title: Boardwalk Empire
description: Atlantic City during Prohibition is ruled by political fixer Nucky Thompson, whose deals with bootleggers put him at the center of a criminal empire.

title: Arliss
description: A fast-talking sports agent navigates the cutthroat world of professional sports representation.

cast.character: Kekee / Card Show Owner / Single Man

title: Chappelle's Show
description: Dave Chappelle hosts this groundbreaking sketch comedy series blending race, pop culture and outrageous humor.

title: Children's Hospital
description: A darkly comic parody of medical dramas set in a Brazilian children's hospital run by emotionally unstable doctors.

title: Colin from Accounts
description: Two strangers are brought together by an injured dog in this sharp Australian romantic comedy.

title: Entourage
description: Film star Vince Chase navigates the vapid terrain of Los Angeles with a close circle of friends and his trusty agent Ari Gold.

title: His & Hers
description: Two estranged spouses — one a detective, the other a news reporter — vie to solve a murder in which each believes the other is a prime suspect.

title: Jury Duty
description: An ordinary man serves on a jury that is secretly a staged mockumentary, unaware that everyone around him is an actor.

title: Key & Peele
description: Keegan-Michael Key and Jordan Peele star in this acclaimed sketch comedy series exploring race, pop culture and American life.

title: Mr. Show with Bob and David
description: Bob Odenkirk and David Cross star in this influential sketch comedy series known for its surreal, interconnected sketches.

title: StartUp
description: A desperate banker, a gang lord who wants to go legit, and a Cuban-American hacker unwittingly create their version of the American dream — organized crime 2.0.

title: The Girlfriend
description: Laura has a perfect life until her son brings home Cherry, a girlfriend who seems to be hiding something dark.

title: The Jeselnik Offensive
description: Anthony Jeselnik hosts this sharp, dark comedy news show with celebrity roast-style humor.

title: The Loop
description: A young airline executive juggles his career ambitions with his chaotic personal life in Chicago.

title: The Righteous Gemstones
description: A famous televangelist family with a long tradition of deviance, greed and charitable work.

title: The Studio
description: The newly appointed head of a movie studio and his executive team juggle corporate demands with creative ambitions as they try to keep movies alive and relevant.

title: There's... Johnny!
description: A coming-of-age story set in 1970s Los Angeles following Andy, a wide-eyed 19-year-old Nebraskan who stumbles his way into a job at The Tonight Show Starring Johnny Carson.

title: Upload
description: A man is uploaded to a digital afterlife, where he begins to question what it means to be human.

title: The Rockford Files
description: Jim Rockford is an easy-going private investigator who lives in a trailer on a Malibu beach, taking on cases the police have given up on.

title: Star Trek
description: The crew of the starship Enterprise explores the galaxy in the 23rd century, seeking out new life and new civilizations.

title: Aerial America
description: Breathtaking aerial footage showcases the stunning landscapes, landmarks and natural wonders of America from coast to coast.

title: The American Revolution
description: A comprehensive documentary series covering the causes, battles and political events of the American Revolution.

title: Baseball
description: Ken Burns' landmark documentary series chronicles the history of America's national pastime from its origins through the modern era.
An English teacher travels back in time to prevent the Kennedy assassination, but discovers he is attached to the life he has made in a bygone era.

title: Friday Night Lights
description: The trials and triumphs of life in the small town of Dillon, Texas, where high school football is everything.

title: High Potential
description: Morgan is a single mom with an exceptional mind whose unconventional knack for solving crimes leads to an unusual partnership with a by-the-book detective.

title: Industry
description: In the cutthroat world of international finance, a group of young graduates compete for a limited set of permanent positions at a top investment bank in London.

title: Ray Donovan
description: Set in the sprawling mecca of the rich and famous, Ray Donovan does the dirty work for LA's top power players and makes their problems disappear. His father's unexpected release from prison sets off a chain of events that shakes the Donovan family to its core.

title: Slow Horses
description: Follow a dysfunctional team of MI5 agents — and their obnoxious boss, the notorious Jackson Lamb — as they navigate the espionage world's smoke and mirrors to defend England from sinister forces.

title: You
description: A dangerously charming, intensely obsessive young man goes to extreme measures to insert himself into the lives of those he becomes fixated on.

title: The Paper
description: The documentary crew that immortalized Dunder Mifflin discovers a historic Toledo newspaper and the eager publisher trying to revive it.

title: The Pitt
description: The staff of Pittsburgh's Trauma Medical Center work around the clock to save lives in an overcrowded and underfunded emergency department.

title: Steal
description: A typical day at Lochmill Capital is upended when armed thieves burst in, and a conflicted detective races to find out who stole 4 billion pounds of peoples pensions.

title: Black Rabbit
description: A rising-star restaurateur is forced into New York's criminal underworld when his chaotic brother returns to town with loan sharks on his trail.

title: The Larry Sanders Show
description: A behind-the-scenes look at a fictional late-night talk show, blurring the line between reality and fiction as host Larry Sanders navigates his insecurities, his staff, and real celebrity guests playing exaggerated versions of themselves.

title: 30 Rock
description: Tina Fey stars as Liz Lemon, head writer of a sketch comedy show at 30 Rockefeller Plaza, navigating her eccentric cast, demanding network executive Jack Donaghy, and the chaos of live television. Won three consecutive Emmy Awards for Outstanding Comedy Series.

title: Animal Control
description: Joel McHale stars as Frank Shaw, a cynical former cop turned senior animal control officer in Seattle. A workplace comedy where the animals are the easy part — it's the humans that complicate everything.

title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
description: Andy Samberg and Andre Braugher lead an ensemble cast of detectives at the fictional 99th Precinct of the NYPD. A warm, funny workplace comedy that won the Golden Globe for Best Comedy Series in its first season.

title: Corporate
description: A pitch-black satire of corporate life following two Junior Executives-in-Training at Hampton DeVille, a soulless multinational conglomerate run by iron-fisted CEO Christian DeVille (Lance Reddick). Described as American Psycho meets Office Space.

title: The Crazy Ones
description: Robin Williams returns to series TV for the first time in 31 years, starring as Simon Roberts, a brilliant but unpredictable advertising genius who runs a Chicago agency with his pragmatic daughter Sydney (Sarah Michelle Gellar). Williams's final television role before his death in 2014.

title: The Dick Van Dyke Show
description: Carl Reiner's landmark comedy follows TV writer Rob Petrie (Dick Van Dyke) balancing his home life in New Rochelle with wife Laura (Mary Tyler Moore) and his work life writing for the fictional Alan Brady Show. 158 episodes across five seasons — one of the most influential sitcoms ever made.

title: The Dick Van Dyke Show Remembered
description: A loving retrospective of The Dick Van Dyke Show hosted by Charles Kuralt, featuring the original cast reunited — Dick Van Dyke, Mary Tyler Moore, Rose Marie, Morey Amsterdam, Ann Morgan Guilbert, Larry Mathews, Carl Reiner, and producer Sheldon Leonard. Aired May 23, 1994 on CBS.

title: Everybody Loves Raymond: 30th Anniversary Reunion
description: The cast of Everybody Loves Raymond reunites 30 years after the show first aired, celebrating the legacy of one of CBS's most beloved sitcoms with Ray Romano, Patricia Heaton, Brad Garrett, Doris Roberts, and Peter Boyle remembered by his castmates.

title: Friends
description: Six friends navigate life, love and careers in New York City. One of the most popular sitcoms in television history, starring Jennifer Aniston, Courteney Cox, Lisa Kudrow, Matt LeBlanc, Matthew Perry and David Schwimmer. 10 seasons, 236 episodes.

title: The King of Queens
description: Kevin James stars as Doug Heffernan, a parcel delivery man in Queens living with his sardonic wife Carrie (Leah Remini) and her freeloading father Arthur (Jerry Stiller). A beloved blue-collar comedy that ran for nine seasons on CBS.

title: The Fall and Rise of Reggie Dinkins
description: From Tina Fey and the team behind 30 Rock, Tracy Morgan stars as Reggie Dinkins, a disgraced ex-NFL star trying to rehabilitate his image with the help of an award-winning filmmaker played by Daniel Radcliffe. A mockumentary sports comedy that premiered as NBC's most-watched comedy debut in three years.

title: Maude
description: Norman Lear's landmark spin-off of All in the Family stars Bea Arthur as Maude Findlay, an outspoken liberal woman in suburban New York. One of the most politically daring sitcoms ever made, tackling abortion, alcoholism and feminism. 141 episodes across six seasons.

title: The Jimmy Stewart Show
description: James Stewart's only television series regular role, playing Professor James K. Howard, a small-town college professor navigating academic and family life. 24 episodes aired on NBC's Sunday night schedule.

title: Stranger Things
description: Set in 1980s Hawkins, Indiana, a group of kids encounter supernatural forces and secret government experiments after their friend goes missing. The Duffer Brothers' sci-fi horror phenomenon ran for five seasons and became Netflix's flagship series, widely regarded as one of the greatest shows ever made.

title: The Jetsons
description: Hanna-Barbera's futuristic counterpart to The Flintstones, following the Jetson family in a space-age utopia of flying cars, robot maids and push-button convenience. Originally ran 1962-63, revived 1985-88.

title: The Flintstones
description: The first primetime animated series in TV history, following the Stone Age family of Fred and Wilma Flintstone in the prehistoric town of Bedrock. A Hanna-Barbera classic that ran 166 episodes across six seasons.

title: The Simpsons
description: The longest-running American animated series and primetime scripted TV series, following the dysfunctional Simpson family in Springfield. Created by Matt Groening, the show has been a cultural institution for over three decades.

title: The Cleveland Show
description: Family Guy spin-off following Cleveland Brown after he moves back to his hometown of Stoolbend, Virginia with his new wife Donna and blended family. Ran four seasons and 88 episodes on Fox.

title: Police Squad!
description: Created by Zucker, Abrahams and Zucker (Airplane!), Leslie Nielsen stars as the deadpan Det. Lt. Frank Drebin of Police Squad. Only 6 episodes aired before ABC cancelled it — calling it the most stupid reason ever given for cancellation. The series spawned The Naked Gun film trilogy. TV Guide ranked it #7 on its list of 60 shows Cancelled Too Soon.

title: Sanford
collectionNote: Collection includes: S1E1 (2 copies), S1E2, S1E3, S1E14, S2E1, S2E2, S2E5, S2E8, S2E9, S2E10, S2E11, S2E12
description: Redd Foxx returns as Fred Sanford in this spin-off of Sanford and Son, now living in Las Vegas and running a pawnshop with a new set of friends and neighbors.

title: Mystery Science Theater 3000
description: Joel Hodgson's cult classic in which a human test subject and robot companions (Tom Servo, Crow T. Robot) are trapped aboard a satellite and forced to watch terrible B-movies, keeping their sanity by riffing on the films. Won a Peabody Award in 1993. Originally on KTMA, Comedy Channel, Comedy Central and Sci-Fi Channel.

title: Trial & Error
description: A mockumentary legal comedy following a bright-eyed New York lawyer (Nicholas D'Agosto) who defends eccentric clients in a small Southern town. Season 1 stars John Lithgow as a poetry professor accused of murder. Season 2 stars Kristin Chenoweth as an heiress whose husband turned up stuffed in a suitcase.

title: The Increasingly Poor Decisions of Todd Margaret
description: David Cross stars as Todd Margaret, an American office temp who bluffs his way into running the London sales team for a North Korean energy drink, knowing nothing about British culture or sales. Co-starring Will Arnett and Sharon Horgan. 3 seasons, 18 episodes on IFC.

title: Happy Together
description: Damon Wayans Jr. and Amber Stevens West star as a suburban couple whose quiet life is upended when a young pop star (Felix Mallard) moves in with them. Loosely inspired by Harry Styles living with his producer. One season, 13 episodes.

title: United States of Al
description: Chuck Lorre comedy about the friendship between Riley (Parker Young), a Marine veteran readjusting to life in Ohio, and Al (Adhir Kalyan), the Afghan interpreter who served with his unit and has just arrived in America. Season 2 was praised for its handling of the Afghanistan withdrawal. 2 seasons on CBS.

title: Looney Tunes & Merrie Melodies
description: The complete Golden Age theatrical cartoon collection — 739 Looney Tunes and Merrie Melodies shorts spanning 1930 to 1969, plus special features, documentaries and bonus content.

title: Schoolhouse Rock!
description: The beloved series of three-minute animated musical shorts that taught generations of American children grammar, math, science, history and civics. From 'Conjunction Junction' to 'I'm Just a Bill,' Schoolhouse Rock! aired Saturday mornings on ABC from 1973 to 1985 and was revived multiple times through 2009. Won four Emmy Awards.

titles: A Noun Is a Person, Place or Thing
I'm Gonna Send Your Vote to College
Lolly, Lolly, Lolly Get Your Adverbs Here
Ready or Not, Here I Come

title: Family Guy
description: Seth MacFarlane's irreverent animated comedy following the Griffin family in Quahog, Rhode Island. One of the longest-running animated series in TV history, known for its cutaway gags, pop culture parodies, and outrageous humor. Cancelled twice and brought back by popular demand.

title: A Charlie Brown Christmas
description: Charlie Brown discovers the true meaning of Christmas when he directs the school Christmas play and adopts a small, scraggly tree. The beloved Peanuts holiday special featuring the Vince Guaraldi jazz score that has aired every year since 1965.

title: It's the Great Pumpkin, Charlie Brown
description: Linus waits in the pumpkin patch for the Great Pumpkin while the rest of the Peanuts gang goes trick-or-treating. The classic Halloween special featuring Snoopy as a World War I flying ace.

title: It's Christmastime Again, Charlie Brown
description: Charlie Brown tries to sell Christmas wreaths while Sally struggles to memorize her lines for the Christmas play. A follow-up to the beloved 1965 classic featuring the Peanuts gang in new holiday adventures.

title: Mister Magoo's Christmas Carol
description: The nearsighted Mr. Magoo stars as Ebenezer Scrooge in this animated musical adaptation of Charles Dickens' classic tale. The first animated Christmas special made for American television, featuring original songs.

title: How the Grinch Stole Christmas!
description: The grumpy Grinch attempts to ruin Christmas for the Whos of Whoville by stealing all their decorations and presents. The classic Dr. Seuss adaptation narrated by Boris Karloff, who also voiced the Grinch.

title: Frosty the Snowman
description: A discarded magician's hat brings a snowman named Frosty to life. He befriends a group of children and embarks on an adventure to the North Pole before he can melt. Narrated by Jimmy Durante.

title: Rudolph the Red-Nosed Reindeer
description: The classic Rankin/Bass stop-motion special following Rudolph as he discovers his glowing nose is not a liability but a gift. Featuring Hermey the elf who wants to be a dentist and the Island of Misfit Toys. The longest-running holiday special in television history.

title: Santa Claus Is Comin' to Town
description: The Rankin/Bass stop-motion origin story of Santa Claus, following Kris Kringle from orphan to the world's most beloved gift-giver. Narrated by Fred Astaire, with Mickey Rooney voicing Kris Kringle.

title: The Year Without a Santa Claus
description: Santa decides to skip Christmas after falling ill and losing faith in holiday spirit. Mrs. Claus sends two elves to find proof that people still believe. Features the iconic Heat Miser and Snow Miser brothers.

title: American Dad!
description: Seth MacFarlane's animated sitcom following the Smith family of Langley Falls, Virginia — CIA agent Stan Smith, his wife Francine, daughter Hayley, son Steve, alien Roger, and talking fish Klaus. Known for its sharper political satire and more serialized storytelling than Family Guy.

title: The Adventures of Rocky and Bullwinkle and Friends
description: Jay Ward's classic animated series following the adventures of Rocky the flying squirrel and Bullwinkle the moose, along with recurring segments featuring Dudley Do-Right, Boris and Natasha, Peabody's Improbable History, and Fractured Fairy Tales. Sharp wit and political satire ahead of its time.

title: The Yogi Bear Show
description: Hanna-Barbera's beloved series following Yogi Bear and his sidekick Boo-Boo as they outwit Ranger Smith in Jellystone Park in pursuit of pic-a-nic baskets. Also features segments with Snagglepuss and Yakky Doodle.

title: Aerial Britain
description: Breathtaking aerial footage revealing the stunning landscapes, coastlines, and historic landmarks of Britain from above. Each episode explores a different region, from the Scottish Highlands to the English countryside.

title: Africa's Hidden Kingdoms
description: A stunning nature documentary exploring the lesser-known animals and ecosystems of Africa — from tiny creatures surviving in extreme environments to the hidden behaviors of Africa's most iconic wildlife.

title: Africa's Hunters
description: Follows individual predators — lions, leopards, cheetahs and wild dogs — as they hunt across the African savanna. Each episode profiles a specific animal's unique hunting strategies and challenges of survival.

title: Africa's Predator Zones
description: Explores the distinct predator ecosystems across Africa's most dangerous territories — from the Okavango Delta to the Serengeti — revealing how each zone's unique geography shapes the hunting behavior of its apex predators.

title: Africa's Wild Havens
description: Documents the extraordinary wildlife sanctuaries and conservation areas across Africa, revealing the rich biodiversity thriving within these protected wild havens and the ongoing efforts to preserve them.

title: America in Color
description: Rare archival footage of American history is colorized and restored, bringing pivotal moments from the 1920s through the 1960s to vivid life — from the Great Depression and World War II to the dawn of the space age.

title: America's Hidden Stories
description: Uncovers little-known stories buried in American history through forensic science, newly discovered documents, and expert analysis — reexamining famous events and forgotten figures that shaped the nation.

title: America's Mississippi
description: An epic journey along the full length of the Mississippi River, exploring the natural wonders, history, culture, and communities that have grown along America's most iconic waterway from Minnesota to the Gulf of Mexico.

title: The Real Mad Men of Advertising
description: Goes behind the scenes of Madison Avenue's golden age, revealing the brilliant and often outrageous figures who shaped American consumer culture through iconic ad campaigns of the 1950s and 60s.

title: Apollo's Moon Shot
description: Marking the 50th anniversary of the Apollo 11 moon landing, this series draws on never-before-seen footage and newly discovered materials to chronicle America's race to put a man on the moon.

title: Arabian Seas
description: Explores the extraordinary marine life thriving beneath the surface of the Arabian Sea, from whale sharks and manta rays to rare coral formations, revealing one of the ocean's least-known ecosystems.

title: Arctic Secrets
description: Ventures deep into the Arctic wilderness to uncover the remarkable wildlife and landscapes of one of Earth's most extreme environments, from polar bears and narwhals to the remote communities that call it home.

title: Atomic Age Declassified
description: Using newly declassified government footage and documents, this series reveals the hidden history of America's nuclear age — from secret weapons tests and Cold War planning to the scientists who built the bomb.

title: Attack and Defend
description: Examines the evolving tactics of military offense and defense throughout history, analyzing how innovations in weapons and strategy have shaped the outcomes of history's most decisive battles.

title: Big Cat Country
description: Follows the lives of lion and leopard families as they compete for territory and survival in the Luangwa Valley of Zambia, one of Africa's most wildlife-rich ecosystems.

title: Brazil Untamed
description: Journeys through Brazil's most remote and biodiverse regions — from the Amazon rainforest to the Pantanal wetlands — revealing the extraordinary wildlife and ecosystems that make Brazil one of the planet's most vital wild places.

title: Britain in Color
description: Using stunning colorization technology, rare black-and-white archival footage of Britain's most defining moments — from the Blitz to the Coronation — is brought to vivid life, revealing the country's 20th century story as never before seen.

title: China from Above
description: Sweeping aerial photography reveals China's extraordinary diversity — ancient landscapes, teeming megacities, remote villages, and spectacular natural wonders — offering a breathtaking bird's-eye view of the world's most populous nation.

title: Combat Trains
description: Chronicles the crucial and often overlooked role of railways in military history, from the Civil War through World War II — revealing how control of the rails often determined the outcome of history's greatest conflicts.

title: Drinks, Crimes and Prohibition
description: Examines the turbulent era of Prohibition through the lens of crime, politics, and social change — revealing how the ban on alcohol unleashed organized crime, corrupted law enforcement, and transformed American society.

title: Eight Days That Made Rome
description: Historian Bettany Hughes relives eight pivotal days that defined the Roman Empire — from the Battle of Zama and Caesar's crossing of the Rubicon to the opening of the Colosseum and the downfall of Nero.

title: Epic Yellowstone
description: Narrated by Bill Pullman and filmed over three years using 8K cameras, drone timelapse, and thermal imagery, this four-part series reveals the predators, prey, and landscapes of America's first national park as never before seen.

title: Extreme Africa
description: Explores Africa's most extreme and hostile environments — scorching deserts, vast floodplains, and impenetrable jungles — revealing how wildlife has adapted to survive in the continent's harshest landscapes.

title: Fishing for Giants
description: South African extreme fisherman Andy Coetzee travels the world on an epic quest to reel in some of the most elusive and iconic giant fish species, from Nile perch to giant trevally.

title: Great Lakes Wild
description: Uncovers the secrets of the vast Great Lakes region — stretching from the Canadian border to the American Midwest — revealing the breathtaking natural beauty and the unique challenges threatening its delicate environmental balance.

title: History in HD
description: Rare and restored historical footage presented in high definition, bringing pivotal moments in world history to vivid life with expert commentary and newly discovered archival material.

title: Humongous Moves
description: Chronicles the extraordinary engineering feats involved in moving some of the world's largest and most difficult objects — from massive ships and historic buildings to giant industrial machines and oversized structures.

title: Killer IQ: Lion vs Hyena
description: Lion whisperer Kevin Richardson and a team of behavioral zoologists pit Africa's two apex predators against each other in a series of groundbreaking cognitive puzzles — from mirror mazes to rope-pulling challenges — to reveal the secrets of predator intelligence.

title: Mighty Trains
description: A journey riding the rails around the world — from the locomotive cabin to rail traffic control to the maintenance depot — exploring the history, engineering, and drama of the planet's most magnificent train routes.

title: Polar Bear Town
description: A half-hour documentary series set in Churchill, Manitoba — the polar bear capital of the world — providing a front-row seat for some of the closest human-bear encounters ever captured on television, as 1,000 polar bears migrate through this small Arctic town each autumn.

title: Secrets
description: Modern historians armed with cutting-edge technology and newly discovered evidence rewrite America's most iconic stories, uncovering the hidden truths behind famous figures, events, and landmarks.

title: Smithsonian Spotlight
description: Short-form documentary segments spotlighting the remarkable artifacts, stories, and scientific discoveries housed within the Smithsonian Institution — the world's largest museum and research complex.

title: Speed Kills
description: Reveals the astonishing science behind nature's fastest predators — from cheetahs and peregrine falcons to sailfish and dragonflies — exploring how speed has evolved as the ultimate survival weapon.

title: Stories From the Vaults
description: Curators and experts from the Smithsonian Institution open the doors to rarely seen collections, revealing the extraordinary objects and untold stories hidden deep within the archives of America's national museum.

title: The Big Dry
description: Documents the devastating effects of prolonged drought on Africa's wildlife and ecosystems, following animals as they struggle to find water and survive in one of the continent's most extreme dry seasons on record.

title: The Lost Tapes
description: Unearthed archival footage and never-before-seen recordings bring history's most pivotal moments to life, revealing the untold stories behind headline events through the raw, unfiltered footage that never made the news.

title: Undiscovered Vistas
description: Stunning aerial and ground-level cinematography explores some of Earth's most remote and rarely visited landscapes, from hidden valleys and ancient forests to uncharted coastlines and volcanic archipelagos.

title: Volcanic Odysseys
description: Journeys to the world's most active and dramatic volcanoes, exploring the raw geological forces that shape our planet while revealing the extraordinary ecosystems and communities that have learned to live in the shadow of these fiery giants.

title: Wild Israel
description: Reveals the remarkable wildlife thriving across Israel's surprisingly diverse landscapes — from the lush forests of the Galilee and the Jordan Valley to the stark Negev Desert and the Red Sea coast.

title: Wild Rockies
description: Explores the extraordinary wildlife and dramatic landscapes of the Rocky Mountains, following grizzlies, wolves, elk, and mountain lions as they navigate one of North America's most spectacular and demanding wilderness regions.

title: Wild, Wild East
description: Ventures into the remote and largely unexplored wilderness of Russia's Far East — home to Amur tigers, brown bears, and rare leopards — revealing one of the planet's last great wild frontiers.

title: World War II: Final Days
description: Using newly declassified footage and intimate accounts from survivors, this series chronicles the dramatic final months of World War II — from the Battle of the Bulge and the fall of Berlin to V-E Day and the atomic bombings that ended the war in the Pacific.

title: Secret Life of the Tasmanian Devil
description: A rare look into the private world of the Tasmanian devil — the world's largest carnivorous marsupial — revealing its surprising family life, hunting behavior, and the devastating facial tumor disease threatening the species with extinction.

title: Secret Life of the Kangaroo
description: Goes inside the hidden world of Australia's most iconic animal, revealing the extraordinary social bonds, surprising intelligence, and remarkable adaptations that have made the kangaroo one of the planet's most successful marsupials.

title: Secret Life of the Koala
description: Reveals the surprising secret life of one of Australia's most beloved animals — exposing the complex social hierarchies, unique communication methods, and survival challenges facing koalas in a rapidly changing environment.

title: Secret Life of the Wombat
description: Uncovers the fascinating hidden behaviors of the wombat — one of Australia's most underrated animals — from their extraordinary underground tunneling systems to their surprisingly complex social lives and unique cubic droppings.

title: Benjamin Franklin
description: Ken Burns explores the revolutionary life of one of history's most consequential personalities — whose 84 years spanned an epoch of change in science, technology, literature, and politics, and who helped invent both electricity and the United States.

title: The Central Park Five
description: Ken Burns, David McMahon and Sarah Burns tell the story of five Black and Latino teenagers wrongly convicted of raping a jogger in Central Park in 1989 — one of the most notorious miscarriages of justice in American history.

title: The Civil War
description: Ken Burns's landmark 9-part documentary covering America's most consequential conflict — from the secession of the Confederacy to the assassination of Lincoln — using photographs, letters, and first-person accounts. Won two Emmy Awards and set records for PBS viewership.

title: The Dust Bowl
description: Ken Burns chronicles the worst man-made ecological disaster in American history — the Dirty Thirties, when the frenzied wheat boom of the Great Plains, followed by a decade-long drought, nearly swept away the breadbasket of the nation.

title: Empire of the Air: The Men Who Made Radio
description: Ken Burns tells the story of three extraordinary men — Lee de Forest, Edwin Howard Armstrong, and David Sarnoff — whose genius, rivalry, and tragedy created the most powerful medium of the 20th century.

title: Hemingway
description: Ken Burns and Lynn Novick's three-part, six-hour examination of the visionary work and turbulent life of Ernest Hemingway — one of the greatest and most influential writers America has ever produced — voiced by Jeff Daniels and Meryl Streep.

title: Jazz
description: Ken Burns's sweeping 10-part, 19-hour history of jazz music — from its roots in New Orleans to bebop, cool jazz, and beyond — celebrating America's greatest original art form through the lives of Louis Armstrong, Duke Ellington, Miles Davis, and dozens more.

title: Leonardo da Vinci
description: Ken Burns, Sarah Burns and David McMahon explore the life of the 15th-century polymath — painter, scientist, engineer, and dreamer — whose thousands of pages of drawings and writings reveal one of humankind's most curious and innovative minds.

title: Muhammad Ali
description: Ken Burns, Sarah Burns and David McMahon bring to life one of the most indelible figures of the 20th century — a three-time heavyweight boxing champion who captivated millions with his combination of speed, grace, and power, and insisted on being himself unconditionally.

title: Prohibition
description: Ken Burns and Lynn Novick tell the story of the rise, rule, and fall of the Eighteenth Amendment — the story of how America tried to ban alcohol, failed spectacularly, gave rise to organized crime, and ultimately came to its senses.

title: The Roosevelts: An Intimate History
description: Ken Burns's seven-part, 14-hour chronicle of Theodore, Franklin and Eleanor Roosevelt — three members of the most prominent family in American politics — voiced by Paul Giamatti, Edward Herrmann, and Meryl Streep.

title: The U.S. and the Holocaust
description: Ken Burns, Lynn Novick and Sarah Botstein examine America's response to one of the greatest humanitarian crises in history — exploring why the United States, which thought of itself as a beacon of freedom, failed to act as six million Jews were murdered in Europe.

title: The Vietnam War
description: Ken Burns and Lynn Novick's ten-part, 18-hour immersive documentary featuring testimony from nearly 80 witnesses — Americans who fought or opposed the war, and Vietnamese combatants and civilians from both sides — exploring one of the most consequential and divisive conflicts in American history.

title: The War
description: Ken Burns's 15-hour documentary on World War II told through the personal stories of ordinary Americans from four towns — Mobile, Sacramento, Waterbury, and Luverne — revealing the human cost of the war on those who fought it and those who waited at home.

title: The League
description: A group of friends in a fantasy football league use the game as an excuse to torment each other, scheme against their wives, and avoid responsibility. An improvisation-heavy comedy about competitive friendships, terrible decisions, and the Shiva Bowl.

title: The Mick
description: Kaitlin Olson plays Mackenzie 'Mick' Murphy, a hard-partying mess who ends up taking care of her wealthy sister's kids in Greenwich, Connecticut after her sister flees the country to avoid prosecution. Chaos and bad parenting ensue.

title: It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia
description: The self-proclaimed Gang — Dennis, Mac, Charlie, Dee, and Frank — run Paddy's Pub in South Philadelphia and routinely engage in shocking, selfish, and morally reprehensible schemes. One of the longest-running live-action comedy series in American TV history.

title: Suits
description: Harvey Specter, Manhattan's top closer, hires Mike Ross — a brilliant college dropout with a photographic memory — as his associate, despite the fact that Mike never went to law school. A sleek legal drama about loyalty, ambition, and keeping secrets.

title: Suits: L.A.
description: A spinoff of Suits set in Los Angeles, following a former federal prosecutor who reinvents himself as a defense attorney and builds a firm that operates by a different set of rules in the high-stakes world of Hollywood law.

title: The Gentlemen
description: Based on Guy Ritchie's 2019 film, this series follows Eddie Horniman who inherits his father's aristocratic estate and discovers it sits on top of a highly profitable marijuana empire — pulling him into the criminal underworld of Britain's upper class.

title: The Rookies
description: Three idealistic young police officers — Gillis, Webster, and Ryker — navigate the realities of law enforcement in a California city under the guidance of their veteran supervisor Lieutenant Ryker. An Aaron Spelling action drama from the early 1970s.

title: Tracker
description: Justin Hartley stars as Colter Shaw, a lone-wolf survivalist who roams the country as a rewards seeker, using his skills to help find missing persons and solve crimes for a fee. CBS's highest-rated drama debut in years.

title: Mayberry R.F.D.
description: The Andy Griffith Show spinoff following widower Sam Jones and his son as they navigate small-town life in Mayberry, North Carolina, with familiar faces including Aunt Bee, Howard Sprague, and Goober Pyle still keeping things lively.

title: Midnight Mass
description: Mike Flanagan's supernatural horror miniseries set on an isolated island community where the arrival of a charismatic young priest brings miraculous events — and terrifying consequences. A meditation on faith, addiction, and mortality.

title: Good Times
description: The Evans family — Florida, James, JJ, Thelma, and Michael — navigate life in a Chicago housing project with humor, warmth, and resilience. The first TV series to feature a two-parent African American family, known for JJ's iconic 'Dy-no-mite!' catchphrase.

title: The Jeffersons
description: George and Louise Jefferson have 'moved on up' to a deluxe apartment on the East Side of Manhattan, thanks to George's chain of dry-cleaning stores. An All in the Family spinoff featuring one of TV's first recurring interracial couples and 11 seasons of sharp social comedy.

title: All in the Family
description: Archie Bunker — the quintessential blue-collar bigot — clashes with his liberal son-in-law Mike 'Meathead' Stivic and navigates a rapidly changing America from his Queens living room. Norman Lear's groundbreaking sitcom that tackled racism, sexism, and politics head-on.

title: Succession
description: The Roy family — Logan and his four children — battle for control of their global media empire Waystar Royco in a darkly comic saga of power, ego, and betrayal. Winner of multiple Emmy Awards for Outstanding Drama Series.

title: Stick
description: A retired special ops soldier turned golf caddie uses his unique skills to navigate the cutthroat world of professional golf — protecting a young prodigy while dealing with dangerous forces both on and off the course.

title: Benson
description: Robert Guillaume's sharp-tongued Benson DuBois leaves the Tate household (from Soap) to become budget director for a bumbling governor, eventually rising to Lieutenant Governor. A smart political sitcom with one of TV's most beloved supporting characters turned lead.

title: Maverick
description: James Garner stars as Bret Maverick — a witty, cowardly, and charming gambler who travels the West with his brother Bart, using brains over brawn to outwit outlaws, con artists, and pompous authority figures. One of TV's first anti-hero Westerns.

title: Bret Maverick
description: James Garner reprises his beloved role as Bret Maverick, now settling down in the town of Sweetwater, Arizona as a saloon owner — but still finding himself drawn into schemes, gunfights, and comic misadventures despite his best intentions.

title: The New Maverick
description: The TV movie pilot that introduced Young Maverick — Ben Maverick, nephew of Bret and Bart — played by Charles Frank. James Garner and Jack Kelly reprise their original roles in this reunion film that launched the short-lived Young Maverick series.

title: Gunsmoke
description: The longest-running Western in American TV history — 20 seasons following U.S. Marshal Matt Dillon, his deputy Festus, saloon owner Miss Kitty, and Doc Adams as they keep the peace in Dodge City, Kansas. James Arness defined the role for two decades.

title: The Blacklist
description: James Spader plays Raymond 'Red' Reddington — one of the FBI's most wanted fugitives — who surrenders and offers to help catch the world's most dangerous criminals, but only if he can work with rookie profiler Elizabeth Keen. Ten seasons of cat-and-mouse intrigue.

title: Don Rickles: Alive and Kicking
description: Don Rickles's 1972 comedy special — a ratings extravaganza featuring Johnny Carson doing the introduction, followed by Rickles working the crowd and a series of sketches with an all-star cast. Features Juliet Prowse in musical numbers alongside the comedy.

title: Don Rickles: One Night Only
description: A star-studded tribute to the King of Insult Comedy, celebrating Don Rickles with roasts, performances, and appearances from decades of Hollywood friends who gathered to honor one of comedy's all-time greats.

title: Rickles
description: A documentary portrait of Don Rickles — the undisputed King of Insult Comedy — featuring rare archival footage and intimate interviews with the comedians, actors, and friends who loved and were scorched by him.

title: Mr. Warmth: The Don Rickles Project
description: John Landis directs this Emmy-winning documentary following Don Rickles on tour, capturing his legendary stand-up act alongside candid moments with celebrity admirers including Robert De Niro, Bob Newhart, and Martin Scorsese.

title: Rickles on the Loose
description: Don Rickles in an intimate stand-up special filmed at the Riviera Hotel in Las Vegas — working the crowd with his signature rapid-fire insults and razor-sharp wit in pure live performance form.

title: The Many Sides of Don Rickles
description: Don Rickles' first one-hour prime-time ABC comedy-variety special, aired September 17, 1970. Featured show guests Don Adams, Robert Goulet, and Harvey Korman in sketches showcasing Rickles at the peak of his insult comedy powers.

title: Don Rickles: Live in Casino Rama
description: Don Rickles performs live at Casino Rama in Ontario, Canada, delivering his signature rapid-fire insult comedy to a packed house in one of his later career stand-up specials.

title: Sunday Best: The Untold Story of Ed Sullivan
description: The untold story of Ed Sullivan — the stone-faced impresario who transformed American entertainment by giving the Beatles, Elvis, and thousands of other performers their biggest stage.

title: I'm Chevy Chase and You're Not
description: A documentary portrait of the complex, combustible, and wildly talented Chevy Chase — from his groundbreaking years at Saturday Night Live to his turbulent Hollywood career and difficult personal life.

title: Carrie Fisher: Wishful Drinking
description: Carrie Fisher's one-woman Broadway show turned HBO special — a brilliantly funny and brutally honest account of her extraordinary life as the daughter of Eddie Fisher and Debbie Reynolds, and her own fame as Princess Leia.

title: Barbara Walters: Tell Me Everything
description: A revealing documentary portrait of Barbara Walters — the trailblazing journalist who shattered barriers in television news, conducted the most coveted interviews of her era, and became one of the most powerful figures in American media.

title: When We Went Mad
description: The definitive account of the making of Mel Brooks' Young Frankenstein — one of the greatest comedies ever made — told through newly unearthed footage and intimate interviews with the surviving cast and crew.

title: Gone Before His Time: Freddie Prinze Sr.
description: The tragic story of Freddie Prinze — the blazing young star of Chico and the Man who rose to fame at 19 and died at 22 — exploring his meteoric rise, personal struggles, and the legacy he left behind.

title: Starring Dick Van Dyke
description: A loving documentary celebration of Dick Van Dyke's remarkable 70-year career — from his Broadway beginnings and Bye Bye Birdie to The Dick Van Dyke Show, Mary Poppins, and his enduring status as a national treasure.

title: Mr. Saturday Night
description: The inside story of Don Rickles and Johnny Carson's 30-year friendship — the most unlikely and enduring bond in show business, told through archival footage and intimate testimony from those who knew them best.

title: Yacht Rock: A Dockumentary
description: The definitive story of Yacht Rock — the smooth, sun-drenched sound of the late 1970s and early 80s that launched a thousand sailboats — featuring the musicians behind the era's most beloved hits.

title: Man on the Run
description: The extraordinary story of O.J. Simpson — from his record-breaking football career and Hollywood crossover to the trial of the century and its aftermath — examining one of the most fascinating and polarizing figures in American history.

title: Louis Armstrong's Black & Blues
description: An intimate portrait of jazz legend Louis Armstrong drawing on never-before-heard home recordings that reveal the private man behind the public persona — his complex feelings about race, his loves, and his extraordinary artistic legacy.

title: Stiller & Meara: Nothing Is Lost
description: A documentary portrait of comedy duo Jerry Stiller and Anne Meara — their partnership on stage and off, their decades-long careers, and the love story that produced Ben Stiller and Amy Stiller.

title: Paul Anka: His Way
description: An intimate documentary following Paul Anka — the teen idol who wrote My Way for Sinatra, Puppy Love, and dozens of other hits — examining his seven-decade career and his unique place in the history of American popular music.

title: Liza: A Truly Terrific Absolutely True Story
description: A documentary portrait of Liza Minnelli — daughter of Judy Garland and Vincente Minnelli, Oscar winner, Broadway legend, and one of the greatest entertainers of the 20th century — told through rare archival footage and personal testimony.

title: The Boys: The Sherman Brothers' Story
description: The story of Richard and Robert Sherman — the songwriting brothers behind Mary Poppins, The Jungle Book, and dozens of other Disney classics — and the complicated personal relationship behind their extraordinary musical partnership.

title: Super/Man: The Christopher Reeve Story
description: The definitive documentary on Christopher Reeve — the actor who defined Superman for a generation — his devastating horseback riding accident, his remarkable advocacy for spinal cord research, and the love story with Dana Reeve that sustained him.

title: Lucy and Desi
description: Amy Poehler directs this intimate documentary about Lucille Ball and Desi Arnaz — the most powerful couple in Hollywood history — exploring their extraordinary creative partnership, turbulent marriage, and the empire they built together.

title: My Mom Jayne
description: Mariska Hargitay's documentary about her mother Jayne Mansfield — the blonde bombshell who was both Hollywood superstar and devoted mother — exploring her life, legacy, and the tragedy that defined her story.

title: John Candy: I Like Me
description: A tender documentary portrait of John Candy — the beloved Canadian comedian and actor who brought joy to millions through films like Planes, Trains and Automobiles and Uncle Buck — exploring the man behind the laughter.

title: Downey Wrote That?
description: A documentary exploring the extraordinary songwriting legacy of Robert Downey Sr. — the overlooked genius behind some of popular music's most beloved songs.

title: Joan Rivers: A Piece of Work
description: An intimate fly-on-the-wall documentary following Joan Rivers through a year of her life — her relentless work ethic, her fear of an empty calendar, and her fierce determination to stay relevant after decades in show business.

title: Groucho & Cavett
description: An American Masters documentary exploring the unlikely friendship between Groucho Marx and Dick Cavett — two of comedy's greatest minds — through rare footage from their extraordinary conversations.

title: Being Eddie
description: A documentary portrait of Eddie Murphy — from his explosive arrival on Saturday Night Live through Beverly Hills Cop, Raw, and his triumphant comeback — examining one of comedy's most electrifying careers.

title: Robin Williams: Come Inside My Mind
description: Marina Zenovich's intimate documentary portrait of Robin Williams — using his own words and the voices of those who knew him best to explore the brilliant, restless, and ultimately tragic mind of one of comedy's greatest talents.

title: Always at the Carlyle
description: A documentary portrait of the legendary Carlyle Hotel in New York City — the home away from home for the world's most celebrated artists, politicians, and royalty — told through the stories of its remarkable guests and staff.

title: What Happened, Miss Simone?
description: Liz Garbus's Emmy-winning documentary on Nina Simone — the classically trained pianist who became a towering figure of the civil rights movement — drawing on never-before-seen footage and her personal diaries.

title: The Aristocrats
description: One hundred of the world's greatest comedians — including Jerry Seinfeld, Robin Williams, and Billy the Kid — tell their version of the world's most notorious and unprintable dirty joke in this celebrated documentary about the art of comedy.

title: Talking Funny
description: Jerry Seinfeld, Ricky Gervais, Chris Rock, and Louis C.K. sit down for an unfiltered conversation about the craft of stand-up comedy — what makes something funny, how to find your voice, and the relentless pursuit of the perfect joke.

title: Johnny Carson: King of Late Night
description: An American Masters documentary chronicling the extraordinary 30-year reign of Johnny Carson as host of The Tonight Show — the most powerful man in American entertainment and the most private public figure of his era.

title: Sammy Davis Jr.: I've Gotta Be Me
description: An American Masters documentary examining the extraordinary life of Sammy Davis Jr. — the Rat Pack member who overcame racism to become one of the most versatile entertainers in history — and his complex relationship with Black America.

title: Sinatra in Palm Springs
description: An intimate documentary exploring Frank Sinatra's deep connection to Palm Springs — the desert retreat where he built his dream home, entertained the Rat Pack, and found refuge from the spotlight — featuring rare photographs and personal memories.

title: Sinatra: All or Nothing at All
description: Alex Gibney's four-hour documentary on Frank Sinatra — using only archival footage and recordings, no talking heads — presenting the full sweep of his extraordinary life and career from Hoboken to Hollywood legend.

title: This Is Bob Hope
description: A documentary portrait of Bob Hope — America's most beloved entertainer for six decades — exploring his extraordinary career entertaining troops, his television dominance, and his complex legacy as a comedic and cultural icon.

title: Rather
description: A documentary portrait of Dan Rather — the legendary CBS News anchor whose 44-year career included covering every major story from the Kennedy assassination to 9/11 — and his controversial final years at the network.

title: Ethel
description: Rory Kennedy's intimate documentary about her mother Ethel Kennedy — widow of Robert F. Kennedy — revealing the remarkable woman behind the Kennedy legend through home movies, family memories, and her own extraordinary voice.

title: The Bee Gees: How Can You Mend a Broken Heart
description: Frank Marshall's documentary on the Bee Gees — Barry, Robin, and Maurice Gibb — tracing their journey from child performers in Australia to disco royalty, exploring the triumphs, tragedies, and towering musical legacy of one of history's greatest pop groups.

title: American Prince: JFK Jr.
description: A documentary examining the life of John F. Kennedy Jr. — America's prince, raised in the glare of history after his father's assassination — from his childhood in the White House to his own tragic death at 38.

title: 1971: The Year That Music Changed Everything
description: A documentary series exploring how 1971 became the most revolutionary year in music history — the year that gave us albums by David Bowie, Marvin Gaye, Carole King, The Rolling Stones, and dozens more that defined a generation.

title: Albert Brooks: Defending My Life
description: Rob Reiner's affectionate documentary on his childhood friend Albert Brooks — one of the most influential comedic minds in American history — featuring intimate conversations and tributes from admirers including James L. Brooks, Judd Apatow, and Larry David.

title: Caddyshack: The Inside Story
description: The definitive behind-the-scenes story of Caddyshack — the 1980 golf comedy that became a cult classic — exploring the chaotic production, the legendary improvisations, and how Chevy Chase, Bill Murray, and Rodney Dangerfield made one of the funniest films ever.

title: Steve Martin: A Documentary in 2 Pieces
description: A two-part documentary portrait of Steve Martin — standup revolutionary, Emmy-winning playwright, Oscar-nominated actor, and banjo virtuoso — exploring the brilliant and restless mind behind one of comedy's most extraordinary careers.

title: Buy Now: The Shopping Conspiracy
description: An exposé of the machinery behind modern consumerism — how corporations, algorithms, and influencer culture conspire to keep us buying things we don't need, and the environmental and psychological toll of our addiction to consumption.

title: Breakdown: 1975
description: A documentary examining the pivotal year of 1975 — when America was reeling from Vietnam, Watergate, and economic collapse — and how that moment of national breakdown planted the seeds for the cultural and political divisions of today.

title: Cruel and Unusual
description: A documentary examining the treatment of transgender prisoners in the American prison system — following five individuals whose stories reveal the systemic cruelty and indifference faced by one of society's most vulnerable populations.

title: Kennedy, Sinatra and the Mafia
description: A documentary exploring the shadowy connections between JFK, Frank Sinatra, and organized crime — examining how the most powerful figures in American politics, entertainment, and the underworld were dangerously intertwined.

title: The Elephant in the Living Room
description: A documentary exploring America's exotic pet trade — following an Ohio wildlife officer and a lion owner whose lives become intertwined — raising urgent questions about the laws that allow dangerous wild animals to be kept as household pets.

title: The Bridge
description: A haunting documentary filmed on the Golden Gate Bridge over the course of a year, capturing the lives and stories of those who jumped — and the family members, friends, and witnesses left behind — sparking urgent conversation about mental health and suicide prevention.

title: JFK: What the Doctors Saw
description: The doctors who were in Trauma Room One at Parkland Memorial Hospital on November 22, 1963 speak publicly for the first time together about what they witnessed — revealing details that challenge the official record of President Kennedy's assassination.

title: The Kennedy Assassination: 24 Hours After
description: A minute-by-minute account of the 24 hours following President Kennedy's assassination — the chaos, the grief, the swearing-in of Lyndon Johnson, and the capture of Lee Harvey Oswald.

title: The Day Kennedy Died
description: A comprehensive documentary examining November 22, 1963 — reconstructing the events of that fateful day hour by hour using newly discovered footage, eyewitness accounts, and forensic analysis.

title: Carville: Winning Is Everything, Stupid
description: An intimate portrait of legendary Democratic strategist James Carville — the Ragin' Cajun who masterminded Bill Clinton's 1992 presidential victory — as he confronts a Democratic Party he barely recognizes.

title: MLK: The Assassination Tapes
description: Using newly uncovered audio recordings and archival footage, this documentary reconstructs the events surrounding the assassination of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. on April 4, 1968 in Memphis, Tennessee.

title: Jimmy Carter: Rock & Roll President
description: A documentary exploring Jimmy Carter's deep and lifelong love of music — and how the 39th President used rock and roll, country, and gospel to connect with the American people and define his presidency.

title: The Sixties
description: Tom Hanks and Gary Goetzman's documentary series chronicling the tumultuous 1960s — the decade of JFK, Vietnam, civil rights, the moon landing, and the counterculture revolution that transformed America.

title: The Seventies
description: A documentary series examining the 1970s — the decade of Watergate, the energy crisis, disco, Vietnam's end, and the birth of the modern political landscape that shaped America today.

title: The Eighties
description: A documentary series exploring the 1980s — the Reagan revolution, the AIDS crisis, the Cold War's end, MTV, and the decade that reshaped American politics, culture, and economics.

title: The Nineties
description: A documentary series examining the 1990s — the Clinton years, the first Gulf War, the Oklahoma City bombing, the rise of the internet, and the cultural explosion that defined Generation X.

title: The 2000s
description: A documentary series covering the first decade of the 21st century — 9/11, the Iraq War, Hurricane Katrina, the rise of social media, and the financial crisis that reshaped the modern world.

title: The 2010s
description: A documentary series examining the most recent decade — the Obama and Trump presidencies, the rise of smartphones, the #MeToo movement, Black Lives Matter, and the fracturing of American consensus.

title: The Bush Years: Family, Duty, Power
description: A documentary series examining the Bush political dynasty — from Prescott Bush through George H.W. and George W. — exploring how one family shaped American conservatism and foreign policy across six decades.

title: 1968: The Year That Changed America
description: A documentary series examining the most turbulent year in modern American history — the assassinations of MLK and RFK, the Vietnam War's peak, the Chicago riots, and the election that set America on its current course.

title: History of the Sitcom
description: An eight-part documentary series tracing the evolution of the American sitcom from I Love Lucy to Seinfeld to The Office — exploring how this uniquely democratic art form has both reflected and shaped American culture.

title: The Story of Late Night
description: A documentary series chronicling the history of late-night television — from Steve Allen and Jack Paar through Carson, Letterman, and Conan — and how the late-night talk show became one of America's most powerful cultural institutions.

title: Kennedy's Suicide Bomber
description: The extraordinary true story of Richard Paul Pavlick — the man who came within days of assassinating President-elect John F. Kennedy in December 1960, a plot that has been largely forgotten by history.

title: The Kennedy Detail
description: The Secret Service agents assigned to protect President Kennedy speak publicly for the first time about the events of November 22, 1963 — their training, their failures, and the guilt they carried for the rest of their lives.

title: Adventures on the New Frontier
description: A Robert Drew Associates cinema verite documentary following President Kennedy and his team during the early days of his administration — one of the first films to capture an American president at work with intimate, unscripted access.

title: Faces of November
description: A haunting documentary capturing the faces of ordinary Americans mourning President Kennedy in the days following his assassination — a time capsule of national grief assembled from footage shot across the country.

title: Crisis: Behind a Presidential Commitment
description: Robert Drew's landmark cinema verite documentary capturing President Kennedy and Attorney General Robert Kennedy navigating the University of Alabama integration crisis in June 1963 — one of the defining moments of the civil rights era.

title: Primary
description: Robert Drew's revolutionary 1960 documentary following John F. Kennedy and Hubert Humphrey through the Wisconsin Democratic primary — the film that invented modern political documentary filmmaking and the fly-on-the-wall style.

title: Robert Drew Associates at the Museum of Tolerance
description: A compilation and retrospective of Robert Drew's groundbreaking cinema verite films — the Kennedy documentaries and beyond — presented at the Museum of Tolerance and examining their lasting impact on documentary filmmaking.

title: The Lost JFK Tapes: The Assassination
description: Newly discovered audio and visual recordings from November 22, 1963 are assembled to reconstruct the Kennedy assassination with unprecedented detail — filling in gaps that have fueled decades of speculation and conspiracy theories.

title: Aftershock: Beyond the Civil War
description: A documentary examining the turbulent Reconstruction era following the Civil War — exploring how the promises of emancipation were systematically dismantled through violence, political betrayal, and the rise of the Ku Klux Klan, with consequences that echo to the present day.

title: Gidget
description: Sally Field's breakout role as Frances 'Gidget' Lawrence — the irrepressible teenage surfer girl navigating boys, school, and life in Malibu. Though it ran only one season, the show launched Sally Field's career and became a beloved classic.

title: Minx
description: Set in 1970s Los Angeles, a feminist writer teams up with a low-rent pornographer to create the first erotic magazine for women. A sharp, funny, and surprisingly heartfelt comedy about ambition, compromise, and female liberation.

title: Party Down
description: A catering company staffed entirely by failed and aspiring Hollywood hopefuls navigates humiliating gigs while dreaming of their big breaks. One of the sharpest, most underrated comedies ever made — revived after a decade for a third season.

title: Loudermilk
description: Ron Livingston plays Sam Loudermilk — a recovering alcoholic and addiction counselor with a razor-sharp tongue and zero patience for stupidity — navigating sobriety, his group, and his complicated past in Seattle.

title: Veep
description: Julia Louis-Dreyfus plays Selina Meyer — Vice President, then President, then perpetually grasping for relevance — in Armando Iannucci's brutally funny satire of American political ambition, vanity, and incompetence.

title: Don't Trust the B---- in Apartment 23
description: A naive Midwestern girl moves to New York City and ends up rooming with a scheming, amoral party girl — and somehow they become best friends. Features James Van Der Beek playing a brilliantly self-aware version of himself.

title: Ugly Americans
description: An animated comedy set in a version of New York City overrun with monsters, zombies, and demons — all of whom need social services. A caseworker tries to help them integrate while navigating his own chaotic relationships.

title: Workaholics
description: Three college dropouts who are also co-workers and housemates stumble through life in Rancho Cucamonga, California — smoking weed, avoiding responsibility, and getting into increasingly absurd situations at their telemarketing job.

title: My Three Sons
description: Fred MacMurray stars as widower Steve Douglas raising three sons with the help of his father-in-law Bub and later his brother-in-law Uncle Charley. One of the longest-running sitcoms in American television history.

title: Newhart
description: Bob Newhart plays Dick Loudon, a New York writer who moves to a Vermont inn with his wife Joanna — only to find the locals completely eccentric. Famous for one of the greatest series finales in television history.

title: The New Adventures of Old Christine
description: Julia Louis-Dreyfus won an Emmy for her role as Christine Campbell — a divorced gym owner navigating single motherhood, her ex-husband's new girlfriend (also named Christine), and her wonderfully dysfunctional family.

title: Royal Pains
description: A disgraced ER doctor becomes a concierge physician to the ultra-wealthy in the Hamptons alongside his wheeler-dealer brother. A breezy, warm-hearted dramedy that ran for eight seasons.

title: Mork & Mindy
description: Robin Williams exploded onto television as Mork — an alien from the planet Ork sent to study human behavior — living with Mindy McConnell in Boulder, Colorado. Williams' improvised brilliance made this one of the most electric sitcoms of its era.

title: Taxi
description: The cabbies of the Sunshine Cab Company in New York City — including Judd Hirsch, Danny DeVito, Andy Kaufman, and Christopher Lloyd — navigate their dreams and disappointments in this Emmy-winning ensemble comedy.

title: The Odd Couple
description: Jack Klugman and Tony Randall star as Oscar Madison and Felix Unger — the original mismatched roommates — in the beloved sitcom adaptation of Neil Simon's play. Their chemistry remains one of TV's all-time great comic partnerships.

title: The Odd Couple
description: Matthew Perry and Thomas Lennon star as Oscar and Felix in this reboot of the classic Neil Simon comedy — updating the mismatched roommates for the digital age while honoring the original's sharp wit.

title: Euphoria
description: Sam Levinson's visually stunning and emotionally raw drama following a group of teenagers navigating identity, trauma, addiction, and love in a Southern California suburb. Zendaya won the Emmy for Outstanding Lead Actress.

title: Monsters
description: Ryan Murphy's anthology series exploring the lives of real-life monsters — beginning with the Menendez brothers and the Menendez family murders — examining how violence, privilege, and the media intersect.

title: Columbo
description: Peter Falk's rumpled, seemingly bumbling Lieutenant Columbo solves murders in Los Angeles — always getting his man through persistence, sharp observation, and that famous 'just one more thing.' One of television's most beloved detective characters.

title: The Twilight Zone
description: Rod Serling's landmark anthology series exploring the darkest corners of the human imagination — each episode a self-contained journey into science fiction, horror, and morality that still resonates as one of the greatest achievements in television history.

title: Mike Tyson Mysteries
description: Mike Tyson, his adopted Korean daughter, a ghost, and a talking pigeon solve mysteries together in this absurdist animated comedy that is exactly as ridiculous as it sounds — and completely delightful.

title: The Zen Diaries of Garry Shandling
description: Judd Apatow's four-hour documentary portrait of the late Garry Shandling — creator of It's Garry Shandling's Show and The Larry Sanders Show — drawing on his personal journals, meditation practice, and the testimony of the comedians he mentored and inspired.

title: Love, American Style
description: An anthology comedy series featuring celebrity guest stars in multiple short romantic vignettes each episode — covering every comic angle on love and romance. Notable for launching the Happy Days pilot and featuring early appearances by Harrison Ford, Diane Keaton, and Sally Struthers.

episodes.title: Love and the Couple of Couples / Love and the Hustler / Love and the Pill
episodes.desc: Love and the Couple of Couples: An ex-wife makes matters difficult for her former husband about to give his new fiancee her ring. Love and the Hustler: An ace pool player meets a lovely novice who chalks up a cue. Love and the Pill: How to get your daughter to take the Pill on her swinger's tour of Europe.

episodes.title: Love and the Living Doll / Love and the Letter / Love and the Joker / Love and the Unlikely Couple
episodes.desc: Love and the Living Doll: A scheme to gain the attentions of a pretty neighbor. Love and the Letter: An immigrant falls for his teacher. Love and the Joker: About a lovestruck practical joker. Love and the Unlikely Couple: A story of two puzzled parents.

episodes.title: Love and the Phone Booth / Love and the Doorknob
episodes.desc: Love and the Phone Booth: A shy country boy tries to date a girl whose number he finds on a phone booth wall. Love and the Doorknob: A doorknob is a source of consternation for newlyweds.

episodes.title: Love and the Legal Agreement / Love and the Militant / Love and Who?
episodes.desc: Love and the Legal Agreement: A separated couple with unseparated housing arrangements. Love and the Militant: A college president confronted by a militant with designs on the secretary. Love and Who?: Fred's New Year's Day hangover headache includes a new marriage license.

episodes.title: Love and the Modern Wife / Love and the Phonies / Love and the Single Couple
episodes.desc: Love and the Modern Wife: A husband disgruntled at his wife's modern marriage course. Love and the Phonies: Putting on airs at a party can't fool the phony-detectors. Love and the Single Couple: What to do when your coed daughter has a male roommate.

episodes.title: Love and the Watchdog / Love and the Busy Husband / Love and the Dating Computer
episodes.desc: Love and the Watchdog: Puff-Puff is a watchdog who's more trouble than he's worth to reunited divorcees. Love and the Busy Husband: How to get the attentions of a busy husband. Love and the Dating Computer: A mixed-up match from a computer dating service.

episodes.title: Love and Take Me Along / Love and the Advice Givers / Love and the Geisha
episodes.desc: Love and Take Me Along: Ozzie Nelson as an unmarried reverend; Harriet as his single companion. Love and the Advice Givers: Well-intentioned advice and the problems it can lead to. Love and the Geisha: How an old flame can kindle a fire in a marriage.

episodes.title: Love and the Wild Party / Love and the Roommate / Love and the Burglar
episodes.desc: Love and the Wild Party: The dancing is anything but square at a swinging party attended by two hayseeds. Love and the Roommate: A roving bachelor falls in love with his girlfriend's roommate. Love and the Burglar: A burglar and the lady he victimizes.

episodes.title: Love and the Former Marriage / Love and the Good Deal / Love and the Big Lead
episodes.desc: Love and the Former Marriage: John Filmore's ex-wife still calls him in every emergency. Love and the Good Deal: The hassle of finding a special bed for a tiny apartment. Love and the Big Lead: A tale about a sky-diving wedding.

episodes.title: Love and Mother / Love and the Dummies / Love and the Athlete / Love and the Shower
episodes.desc: Love and Mother: Three is a crowd when a new bride's mother shows up. Love and the Dummies: Two shy ventriloquists let their dummies do all the talking. Love and the Athlete: How to tell a lady athlete that she's really a man. Love and the Shower: A divorce shower for the newly single man.

episodes.title: Love and the Comedy Team / Love and the Divorce Sale / Love and the Mountain Cabin
episodes.desc: Love and the Comedy Team: Two comedy writers try to meet a deadline but one's fiancee is not very understanding. Love and the Divorce Sale: The end of a marriage is marked by a divorce sale. Love and the Mountain Cabin: A mountain-retreat honeymoon, but hubby's mind is on the money hidden there.

episodes.title: Love and the Bachelor / Love and the Other Love / Love and the Positive Man
episodes.desc: Love and the Bachelor: A bachelor lays down the law to his overprotective mother. Love and the Other Love: Rob's all-consuming love for his new sports car strains his marriage. Love and the Positive Man: Five-foot-three Marvin aspires to win the heart of the statuesque Sylvia.

title: The Adventures of Ozzie and Harriet
description: America's favorite family — bandleader Ozzie Nelson, his wife Harriet, and their sons David and Ricky — in the longest-running live-action sitcom in American TV history. One of television's first reality-adjacent shows, starring the actual Nelson family as themselves.

title: Mythic Quest
description: The chaotic team behind the world's most popular online game — led by narcissistic creative director Ian Grimm — navigate the absurdities of the gaming industry. A sharp workplace comedy from the creators of It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia.

title: Red Oaks
description: Set in a New Jersey country club in the summer of 1985, a college student working as a tennis pro navigates his uncertain future, his complicated family, and an unexpected romance. A warm, funny coming-of-age story produced by Steven Soderbergh.

title: Rowan & Martin's Laugh-In
description: The wildly innovative comedy-variety series that changed television forever.
